message:
fix(ffs-block): replace S3-FIFO cache panics with self-healing invariant recovery

The S3-FIFO eviction cache previously panicked on invariant violations
(ghost hits, stale metadata, rebalance exhaustion), which made the
filesystem fragile under concurrent workloads with dirty blocks.

This overhaul replaces every panic path with graceful recovery:

- s3_on_hit: ghost/unknown location hits now repair metadata and log
  warnings instead of crashing; small caches (<=32 slots) use eager
  T1-to-T2 promotion on hit for better adaptation
- s3_on_miss_or_ghost_hit: stale resident-key misses now drop the
  corrupt loc/access_count entries and continue through normal miss
  admission rather than incorrectly treating them as hits
- s3_rebalance_queues: added pending_admission tracking to prevent
  evicting the block about to be admitted; emergency/pressure fallback
  eviction loops for when second-chance rotation is insufficient;
  tolerates overflow when all remaining candidates are dirty (logs
  overflow_tolerated_dirty); last-resort forced clean eviction before
  logging error
- s3_capacity_split: special-case for tiny caches (capacity <= 4) with
  relaxed split targets; p field initialized to 0 instead of
  small_capacity for correct cold-start adaptation
- New s3_reconcile_resident_queues: reconciles T1/T2 queues against the
  actual resident map, removes stale/duplicate entries, rebuilds loc
  metadata, and returns count of repaired entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
